metaTitle: Sustainable Weight Management | Piedmont Nutrition Care
metaDescription: Root-cause metabolic repair for weight-loss resistance. GLP-1 medication off-ramp support. Functional nutrition in Winston-Salem, NC.
headline: You've tried so hard to lose the weight. Let's figure out why it keeps coming back.
subheadline: If you've lost weight and gained it back — more than once — it's not a failure of willpower. Your metabolism, gut health, and hunger hormones all play a role. Piedmont Nutrition Care helps you understand what's been happening and builds a sustainable plan around foods you love.
agitation: You've lost 20 pounds, 30 pounds, maybe more — and gained it all back. Every time feels more discouraging than the last. It's easy to blame yourself, but the truth is: restrictive diets work against your biology, not with it.
Your body has hunger hormones, metabolic set points, and gut bacteria that all influence weight regulation. Without addressing these underlying factors, any diet is fighting an uphill battle.
howItWorks: Jeanne starts with your metabolic baseline: lab markers, hormonal indicators, gut health assessment, and a full history of what you've tried and how your body responded. She identifies the specific disruptions — leptin resistance, gut dysbiosis, insulin sensitivity, cortisol-driven belly fat accumulation — and builds a food chemistry protocol that repairs each one systematically.

For patients on or coming off GLP-1 medications (Ozempic, Wegovy, Mounjaro): Jeanne provides a "Medication Off-Ramp" protocol that rebuilds the gut flora and metabolic resilience your body needs to maintain results after medication ends. This isn't anti-medication. It's ensuring the medication's results are preserved by a biological foundation, not dependent on a continued prescription.

testimonial.quote: Jeanne is extremely knowledgeable about food, food chemistry, and the latest research findings on nutrition. She can suggest dietary changes to prevent cancer, boost a sluggish metabolism, improve blood sugar, or lower LDL.
testimonial.author: Liz

subheadlineShort: Losing and regaining weight isn't a willpower failure. Your metabolism and gut health play a big role. We build a sustainable plan around foods you love.
agitationShort: You've lost the weight before and gained it back. It's discouraging, but restrictive diets work against your biology, not with it.
Hunger hormones, metabolic set points, and gut bacteria all influence weight. Without addressing them, any diet is an uphill battle.
howItWorksShort: Jeanne identifies the specific disruptions — leptin resistance, gut dysbiosis, insulin sensitivity — and builds a protocol that repairs each one systematically.
For patients on or coming off GLP-1 medications: a "Medication Off-Ramp" protocol that rebuilds the metabolic resilience your body needs to maintain results.
